title: derris parviflora
intro: Derris parviflora, also known as the small-flowered derris, is a plant species that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a natural insecticide and to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, providing health benefits such as pain relief and wound healing. Derris parviflora is often prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of a tea, infusion, or decoction, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds.
